title: evolvulus piurensis
intro: Evolvulus piurensis, also known as the blue daze or creeping evolvulus, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its small, blue-violet flowers and sprawling growth habit. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Evolvulus piurensis include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Evolvulus piurensis can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being applied topically to the skin in the form of poultices or salves.
